content:
Weapons and Armour which are good candidates to start crafting on. Elite gear with more than 20 quality (which can only drop, not be crafted), and/or, in the case of Armour, a good Base Defence roll. You can optionally specify the weapon or armour type, and/or the minimum item level. Item Level is an important aspect of crafting in Path of Exile. It determines the maximum Affix Tier that can roll on the item. Visit Craft of Exile to discover which affixes require which Item Level. Item Level is determined by the Area Level, and the type of Monster killed (Magic +1, Rare and Boss monsters +2). Area Level is fixed during each zone in the Campaign, or determined by the Map Tier. It is displayed in the top-right corner of the Game Screen when you have the Minimap open (tab).
The maximum Item Level is 85, only dropped by Rare monsters and Bosses in T16 maps.
Map Tier Area Level Monster Level T1 (White) 68 68 / 69 / 70 T6 (Yellow) 73 73 / 74 / 75 T11 (Red) 78 78 / 79 / 80 T16 (Max) 83 83 / 84 / 85

In high tier maps, Exceptional gear can start to drop. This is Normal gear with either more than 20 Quality, or an extra socket, and thus ideal to craft on. For any 'My Gear' rules you specified, the filter will automatically highlight Exceptional variants. Here you can additionally highlight Exceptional items of all types.

Equipment with a high Item Level (iLevel) are also good candidates to craft on. The iLevel determines the maximum Affix Tier that can roll on the item. Visit Craft of Exile to discover which affixes require which iLevel.

iLevel is determined by the Area Level, and the type of Monster killed (Rare monsters +1, Boss monsters +2). Area Level is fixed during each zone in the Campaign, or determined by the Waystone Tier. Some areas have +1 to Area Level. Area Level is displayed in the top-right corner of the Game Screen when you have the Minimap open (tab).

Example: Tier 15 Waystones have an Area level of 79; Irradiated Tier 15 Waystones get +1 to Area level, so they have an Area level of 80. Rare Monsters in these zones would drop Items of iLevel 81, while Bosses would drop Items of iLevel 82.

Create fine-grained rules for individual items or groups of items.

These rules have a higher precedence than the Drop Tier Styles above, Custom Rules, and Quick Filters, and are processed in the order they are listed. The higher the rule, the higher its precedence.

The maximum level difference between the Item's Base Type's Drop Level and the Area Level. Leave blank to always show these types of Items, regardless of level difference.

For example, if set to 10, then an item of a Base Type with a Drop Level of 20 will be shown up until Areas with level 30. Starting in Areas with level 31 it would no longer be shown.

These settings allow you to change the default style of currency and items that belong to one of the Drop Tiers. You can also change the style by clicking on the cosmetic icon on the header on the corresponding Drop Tier tab.

For Uniques and Chance Bases, disabling the drop tier using the checkbox at the start of the row will disable the corresponding Quick Filter Highlight rule. Note that this does not hide the items, it just doesn't apply the cosmetic styles.

Since Jewels can only have 4 mods, the rules are different:
- Excellent Jewels must have at least 2 Great mods, in total 3 Great or Good mods; and no Bad mods.
- Good Jewels must have at least 1 Great mod, in total 2 Great or Good mods; and no Bad mods.

Chance Bases are Normal versions of items which are good candidates to use an Orb of Chance on. On the Quick Filters tab under the 'Highlight Uniques and Chance Bases' section, you can specify whether you want to highlight Chance Bases you're currently searching. Here you can specify which Chance Bases you currently want to search for.

You can add additional common but often-used class-specific Uniques to a separate 'Potential' tier, from where you can add them to higher tiers. This is especially useful for SSF players. Head over to poe2.ninja for build inspiration! Head over to poe.ninja for build inspiration!

These Gear Tiers are used on the Quick Filters tab in the sections where you can hide gear by Gear Tier, or set 'My Weapons' and 'My Armour' rules. Advanced and Expert Gear Tiers are better versions of their Basic counterparts. Elite are the best of the Expert items. Review and adjust the Elite tier as needed.

Note that Quivers, Wands, Staves and Sceptres don't have improved versions of the Basic Base Types, and thus their categorization is more opinionated. Review and adjust to your needs.

The 'Partial' checkbox at the end of each row enables or disables the 'Continue' statement. By default, Cosmetic Rules will have 'Partial/Continue' enabled. This means that the game will continue looking for more matching rules further down, e.g. add a Drop Tier Highlight (Beam and Minimap Icon). This might override your rules. If you don't want this, and use your own Highlight, you have two options:

  1. If you specify your own Highlight (Beam and/or Minimap Icon) for a rule, you can safely disable its 'Partial' checkbox.
    Note: The tool will automatically disable 'Partial' when you select a Highlight.
    Use this if you only have a few additional Cosmetic Rules with your own Highlights.
  2. Alternatively, you can Disable Drop Tier Highlights (Beam and/or Minimap Icon) of all Drop Tiers using the 'Drop Tier Styles' section above. Then you can keep the 'Partial' rule enabled for all Cosmetic Rules.
    Use this only if you don't care about the Drop Tier Highlights, and/or if you will add your own for all valuable items using Custom Cosmetic Rules (disadvised).
    Pro tip: Are you sure you don't want to just style the Drop Tiers instead using the 'Drop Tier Styles' section above?

Currency, Divination Cards, Uniques and Chance Bases are divided into Drop Tiers, based on their value. An item's value is either determined by its market price in your selected Economy, or a 'hard-coded' value if you're using the 'Fixed (SSF)' Economy. Tiers have a threshold or cutoff value, above which items belong to at least that tier. You can either use one of the preset Tiering Modes (Early-Game, Normal, Strict, Very Strict), or configure your own 'Custom' cutoff values.

When you have any of the game's economies selected (League, HC League, Standard, HC Standard), Currency & Unique values get updated regularly automatically. This can cause items to move up or down one or more tiers. We'll inform you, and the Changelog tab presents you with an overview of these movers.

You can drag & drop items to any other tier. These items will stay 'fixed' in the tier you assigned them to, regardless of market value fluctuations or the Tiering Mode you select. Use the ↻ button to undo your custom tier and return the item to its original tier based on its (market) value.

Currency dropped in a stack of 3 is moved one tier higher, a stack of 10 two tiers higher (configurable on the Quick Filters tab).

Currency values are updated every 4 hours from the in-game Currency Exchange. Uniques values are pulled in every hour from poe.ninja. Currency values are updated every 4 hours from the in-game Currency Exchange. Uniques values are scraped daily from the trade website.

On the Quick Filters tab under the 'Highlight Uniques and Chance Bases' section, you can specify whether you want to highlight potentially Excellent, Good, Potential and/or Other Unique items. Here you can specify which Unique (or rather, its Base Type) you consider Good or Excellent.

A Base Type's Tier is determined by the tier of its most valuable Unique. If a Base Type additionally has other, less valuable Uniques without Drop Restrictions, the Base Type might be moved to the 'Potential' tier:
- For Excellent Uniques: either if the lowest value is less than 10c ex or the average value is less than 20c ex.
- For Good Uniques: either if the lowest value is less than 5c ex or the average value is less than 5c ex.

If you have the 'High Dust Value Uniques' Quick Filter setting enabled, 'Other' and 'Potential' Uniques which have an average size-corrected Thaumaturgic Dust value of at least 15000 and a minimum of at least 10000 among non-restricted Uniques will be promoted to the 'Good' tier. We're using the Item Level 84 and 0 Quality Dust value. Lower iLevel Uniques would yield considerably less Dust. Inversely, don't forget to increase the Quality of the highest-value Uniques before disenchanting them, as this will yield 20% more Dust.

You can add additional common but often-used class-specific Uniques to a separate 'Potential' tier, from where you can add them to higher tiers. This is especially useful for SSF players. Head over to poe2.ninja for build inspiration! Head over to poe.ninja for build inspiration!
We classify Gear Base Types into Basic, Advanced, Expert and Elite tiers, depending how good they are. You can see and change which Base Type belongs to which Tier on the Gear Tier List tab.

Gear is divided into 'Gear Tiers', reflecting how good its Base Type is. Hide all gear which has a Base Type which is Basic, Advanced, Expert or Elite.

You can specify which gear belongs to which Gear Tier on the Gear Tier List tab.

Note that this will also hide Quivers, Wands, Sceptres and Staves which are considered lower tier. To prevent this, include them in the 'My Weapons' section below.

The following flask types will be hidden automatically by the Area Level:

- Lvl 15: Lesser, Medium
- Lvl 25: Grand, Greater
- Lvl 32: Giant
- Lvl 42: Colossal
- Lvl 52: Gargantuan
- Lvl 62: Transcendent
- Lvl 65: Normal Ultimate
- Lvl 70: Ultimate < 10% Quality
- Lvl 75: Ultimate < 15% Quality
- Lvl 80: Ultimate < 20% Quality The following flask types will be hidden automatically by the Area Level:

Mana & Life
- Lvl 15: Small, Medium, Large
- Lvl 30: Greater, Grand
- Lvl 48: Colossal, Giant, Sacred
- Lvl 60: Hallowed, Sanctified

Hybrid
- Lvl 20: Small
- Lvl 30: Medium
- Lvl 40: Large
- Lvl 50: Colossal
- Lvl 60: Sacred
- Lvl 67: Hallowed

When this is enabled, you cannot use the 'Hide Remaining Flasks' filters.

This shows and/or highlights Normal items which are good candidates to use an Orb of Chance on.

You can change which Base Types you're currently looking for or which have 'Potential' on the Chance Base Tier List tab.

You can change the style of the highlights: here, by clicking on the Minimap Icon; on the Chance Base Tier List tab, by clicking on the Tier Header; or on the Chance Base Cosmetics tab.

If you specify multiple conditions, they each apply separately. For example, specifying both 'Hide Normal and Magic' and 'Hide Basic and Advanced' will never show any item which is either Normal or Magic, or Basic or Advanced; so only Expert and Elite items of rarity Rare or higher will still be shown.

This shows or highlights all identified rare items which have any of the content-restricted mods.

- Delve: 'Subterranean', 'of the Underground', and the 4 beast aspect mods: 'of Craiceann', 'of Farrul', 'of Saqawal', 'of Fenumus'

- Incursion: 'Citaqualotl's', 'Guatelitzi's', 'Matatl's', 'Tacati's', 'Topotante's', 'Xopec's', 'of Citaqualotl', 'of Guatelitzi', 'of Matatl', 'of Puhuarte', 'of Tacati'

- Breach: 'Chayula's', 'Esh's', 'Tul's', 'Uul-Netol's', 'Xoph's', 'of Chayula', 'of Esh', 'of Tul', 'of Uul-Netol', 'of Xoph'

Potential Uniques can be good but probably aren't. These Base Types have one or a few (very) valuable Uniques, but at least one common less valuable Unique. Hover over the dropped item to see which Unique it is, and check whether it is a valuable one on the Uniques Tier List tab. Alternatively, use a price checker like Awakened Trade Exiled Exchange 2 after picking it up.

Restricted Uniques are Base Types where all the valuable Uniques are 'Restricted' drops, i.e. they can only drop from a given Boss, area, mechanic, be crafted,... If you find these during general Mapping, they are guaranteed not to be valuable.

You can safely disable and even hide these when you are running regular maps. Be sure to enable this when doing bosses or special content.

Select which Excellent (first input field) and Good (second input field) socket group of linked sockets you are looking for. Excellent socketed gear is shown more prominently than Good socketed gear. You can configure more details using the button.

Enter a string of characters representing linked sockets using the characters Red, Green, Blue, White, and X for any colour. For example, 'RRGX' would select all gear with at least 4 linked sockets, of which two are Red and one is Green. The X can be any colour.

Late in the campaign and while mapping, Rare and Magic equipment with an 'Unidentified Item Tier' can drop, with tiers ranging from (Tier 2) to (Tier 5). The higher the tier, the more likely that the item will have better affixes.

These rules apply to all types of equipment. You can specify more specific rules for equipment you are particularly looking for in 'My Weapons' and 'My Armour' rules, under the 'My Equipment' section.

Note that in general, Magic equipment is considered more valuable than Rare equipment since you can craft on it further. Rare equipment will almost never contain only good modifiers.
